title: Setting up Clean as You Code | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Setting up Clean as You Code for your project

## [Step 1: Set the quality standard for your project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/clean-as-you-code/#setting-your-quality-standard "Step 1: Set the quality standard for your project")

The project's quality standards include:

- Quality gates
  Quality gates enforce a quality policy for the results of code review and analysis in your organization by answering one question: is my project ready for release?
  For more information, see [Understanding quality gates](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/standards/managing-quality-gates/introduction-to-quality-gates/ "Understanding quality gates").
- Quality profiles
  Quality profiles define the set of rules to be applied during code analysis and review. 
  For more information, see [Understanding quality profiles](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/standards/managing-quality-profiles/understanding-quality-profiles/ "Understanding quality profiles").

The default quality gate and quality profiles defined by your Quality Standard administrator apply by default to your project. To change them, see:

- [Changing the quality gate of your project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/changing-quality-gate/ "Changing the quality gate of your project")
- [Changing the quality profiles of your project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/quality-profile-association/ "Changing the quality profiles of your project")

## [Step 2: Set a new code definition for your project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/clean-as-you-code/#setting-a-new-code-definition "Step 2: Set a new code definition for your project")

With the Clean as You Code approach, focus is set on new code. New code is code that you've recently added or modified. The new code definition tells SonarQube which part of the code is considered new during analysis. For more information, see [About new code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/digging-deeper/clean-as-you-code/about-new-code/ "About new code").

When your project is created (whatever the creation method), the new code definition set at the organization level (if any) is applied to your project by default. You can select another new code definition for your project.

See [Configuring the new code calculation for your project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/configuring-new-code-calculation/ "Configuring the new code calculation for your project").

## [Step 3: Set up the analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/clean-as-you-code/#setting-up-the-analysis "Step 3: Set up the analysis")

To learn how to set up analysis in your IDE and with SonarQube Cloud, refer to the following links:

- SonarQube for [IntelliJ](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/ " IntelliJ"), [Visual Studio](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/visual-studio/ "Visual Studio"), [VS Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/ "VS Code"), [Eclipse](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/eclipse/ "Eclipse")
- [Branch analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/branch-analysis/ "Branch analysis")
- [Pull request analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/pull-request-analysis/ "Pull request analysis")

## [Related pages](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/clean-as-you-code/#related-pages "Related pages")

- [Clean as You Code implementation](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/clean-as-you-code/implementation/ "Clean as You Code implementation")
- [Managing the quality gates](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/standards/managing-quality-gates/introduction/ "Managing the quality gates")
- [Managing the quality profiles](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/standards/managing-quality-profiles/introduction/ "Managing the quality profiles")
